channel.title: Sintra Tesseract — AI News
channel.description: Curated AI news: model releases, benchmarks, research breakthroughs, and industry events.

item.title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
item.description: John Jumper, co-creator of AlphaFold and 2024 Nobel Chemistry laureate, announced on June 19, 2026 that he is leaving Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic. AlphaFold has predicted the 3D structures of over 200 million proteins, and Jumper's departure follows Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer's exit to OpenAI by just one day — two landmark talent losses for Google within 48 hours.

item.title: SK Telecom Named as Trigger Behind Anthropic Mythos Export Ban; Anthropic Opens Seoul Office
item.description: Reporting on June 19, 2026 revealed that SK Telecom — South Korea's largest carrier, added to Anthropic's Project Glasswing in early June — had its Claude Mythos access revoked by Anthropic at the White House's request over alleged China ties, an episode that escalated into the June 12 US export-control directive barring foreign nationals from Mythos 5 and Fable 5. SK Telecom denies any China ties, citing roughly $1.9 million in Chinese revenue and seven China-based employees in 2024. Anthropic opened a Seoul office the same week even as the export ban cuts Korean access to its top models.

item.title: European Commission Picks Domyn-Led EUROPA Consortium for Frontier AI Grand Challenge
item.description: The European Commission selected the EUROPA consortium, led by Italian company Domyn, as winner of its Frontier AI Grand Challenge launched in February 2026. EUROPA will build an open-source frontier AI model covering all 24 official EU languages with computational capacity equivalent to at least 400 billion parameters, using efficient Mixture-of-Experts architectures, and will receive up to 2.5% of EuroHPC's total AI-optimized supercomputing capacity for one year.

item.title: EU AI Act Advisory Forum Holds Inaugural Session With 174 Members
item.description: The European Commission hosted the first session of the AI Act Advisory Forum, the general advisory body supporting AI Act implementation and enforcement, with 174 members selected from over 700 applicants across civil society, academia, industry, and SMEs and startups. It runs alongside the separately appointed 60-member Scientific Panel focused on general-purpose AI model classification and systemic-risk evaluation.

item.title: Gemini Co-Lead Noam Shazeer Leaves Google for OpenAI
item.description: Noam Shazeer, co-lead of Google's Gemini model and co-author of the original 2017 'Attention Is All You Need' Transformer paper, announced on June 18, 2026 that he is leaving Google to join OpenAI as Lead for AI Architecture Research, overseeing fundamental model design. The move comes less than two years after Google paid roughly $2.7 billion to bring Shazeer (and Character.AI co-founder Daniel De Freitas) back into DeepMind in August 2024.

item.title: Brazilian Proptech QuintoAndar to Invest R$2 Billion in AI Over Two Years
item.description: Brazilian real-estate unicorn QuintoAndar announced on June 18, 2026, from its new São Paulo headquarters, that it will invest R$2 billion (about $395 million) over two years to rebuild its product around AI, self-funded from company cash. Plans include AI virtual assistants spanning search-to-close, automated customer service, personalized property recommendations, AI-assisted pricing, and AI-supported credit analysis; the company says 80% of its code is already written with AI assistance.

item.title: Inference startup Baseten nears $1.5B raise at $13B valuation
item.description: Baseten is finalizing a $1.5 billion round at up to a $13 billion valuation, up from $5 billion just five months earlier, co-led by Spark Capital, Sands Capital, Altimeter Capital and Wellington Management. The company's annualized revenue grew from roughly $200 million to $600 million in a single quarter, driven by demand for running and optimizing open-source models.

item.title: FERC orders six US grid operators to justify or reform data center connection rules
item.description: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission issued tailored show-cause orders on June 18 to PJM, MISO, SPP, CAISO, ISO-NE and NYISO, giving each 60 days to justify current large-load interconnection tariffs or propose reforms, plus a 30-day deadline to report on generation adequacy. The orders target faster grid access for AI data centers while addressing cost-shifting and co-location rules.

item.title: Macron and Modi headline VivaTech with India as AI country partner
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi appeared together at VivaTech 2026 on June 18, with India serving as the event's official AI Country Partner for its 10th edition. The event drew over 200,000 visitors and is positioned as a venue for tracking Europe's "AI buildout moving to construction phase."

item.title: São Paulo Study Finds AI Moved R$1.7 Billion in the City During 2025
item.description: A São Paulo Negócios (the city's investment-promotion agency) study of 52 respondents — tech companies, consultancies, research institutes, and government bodies — found AI-related activity moved R$1.7 billion ($310M) in the city of São Paulo in 2025, with a calculated R$2.7 billion impact on participating companies' revenue, R$116 million in tax collection, and R$1.5 billion added to municipal GDP. About a third of respondents said São Paulo's AI market is more advanced than the rest of Brazil and Latin America.

item.title: Gemini Co-Lead Noam Shazeer Leaves Google for OpenAI
item.description: Noam Shazeer, co-lead of Google's Gemini models and co-author of the 2017 'Attention Is All You Need' Transformer paper, announced he is leaving Google to join OpenAI. Alphabet shares fell on the news; Sam Altman publicly welcomed him, saying OpenAI had wanted to hire him 'since the very beginning.'

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Access Restored With Nationality-Based Restrictions After Six-Day Outage
item.description: Anthropic restored Claude Fable 5 access roughly six days after the US government's export-control directive forced a global shutdown for foreign nationals, but the restored model now ships with nationality-based access controls and enhanced compliance screening. Developers reported more frequent fallback to Claude Opus 4.8 on cybersecurity, chemistry, and biology prompts; Claude Mythos 5 remained offline, restricted to a limited partner program.

item.title: OpenAI Adds Usage Analytics and Spend Controls to ChatGPT Enterprise
item.description: OpenAI introduced a Global Admin Console feature for ChatGPT Enterprise that surfaces credit consumption across ChatGPT and Codex by user, product, and model, plus configurable workspace credit limits, group-level caps, and individual spend overrides, all available via a unified Cost API. The launch targets enterprise customers facing rising scrutiny over AI spend.

item.title: FERC Issues Show-Cause Orders to All Six US Grid Operators Over AI Data Center Power Connections
item.description: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission issued tailored Section 206 show-cause orders to PJM, MISO, SPP, CAISO, ISO-NE, and NYISO, giving each 60 days to justify or reform tariffs governing how data centers and other large loads connect to the grid, plus 30-day resource-adequacy reporting requirements. FERC said the action affects regions serving roughly 200 million Americans.

item.title: Perplexity Launches 'Brain,' a Self-Improving Memory System for Its Computer Agent
item.description: Perplexity launched Brain, a system that builds a context graph of work performed by its 'Computer' agent product and periodically retrains itself to improve task performance over time. The feature is rolling out in research preview to Perplexity Max and Enterprise Max subscribers.

item.title: Gallup: Tech Workers Who Skip AI Face Triple the Layoff Risk
item.description: A Gallup study based on its Feb. 4–19, 2026 survey of 23,717 US employees (including 660 who reported being unemployed after their jobs were eliminated) found tech workers who use AI at least monthly face a predicted layoff risk of about 6%, versus 18% for those who use it less often — three times higher. Separately, 31% of tech-sector workers believe their job is somewhat or very likely to be eliminated within five years due to technology, AI, or automation, versus 18% of US workers overall.

item.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Sweeps Every MLPerf Training 6.0 Benchmark
item.description: NVIDIA's Blackwell platform topped every category in MLPerf Training v6.0, posting the fastest time-to-train across all benchmarks and the largest-scale submission to date — 8,192 GPUs on GB200 NVL72 systems. New GB300 NVL72 systems delivered up to 1.6x faster training than GB200 at the same rack scale, and NVIDIA set records on two new MoE pretraining workloads added this round, DeepSeek-V3 671B and GPT-OSS-20B.

item.title: OpenAI Releases LifeSciBench, a 750-Task Life-Science Research Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI published LifeSciBench on June 17, 2026, a 750-task benchmark built with 173 PhD-level scientists, grading models on free-response life-science research tasks — including genomic sequence files, chemical structures, and experimental figures — against expert-written rubrics averaging 25 criteria each (19,020 criteria total). The strongest model passed only 36.1% of all tasks and just 28.1% of artifact-heavy tasks, identifying scientific-artifact processing as the primary bottleneck for current AI systems.

item.title: OpenAI publishes "near-autonomous AI chemist" research alongside new lab benchmark
item.description: OpenAI released research on June 17 describing a near-autonomous AI system capable of planning and executing multi-step chemistry experiments with limited human intervention. The publication accompanied a new evaluation framework for measuring AI performance on life-sciences research tasks.

item.title: EU's 2026 State of the Digital Decade report flags AI adoption gap despite 48% growth
item.description: The European Commission's fourth State of the Digital Decade report, published June 17, found AI adoption among EU businesses rose 48% in 2025 but remains under 20%, trailing cloud computing (46.7%) and data analytics (39.9%) adoption. The report warns that structural gaps in compute capacity, skills and scale-up funding could cause the EU to miss its 2030 digital targets.

item.title: Mistral AI and Nvidia launch "Mistral Compute" sovereign cloud at VivaTech
item.description: At VivaTech Paris, Mistral AI and Nvidia confirmed the launch of Mistral Compute, a sovereign European AI infrastructure and GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 Grace Blackwell Superchips, partially operational at the Bruyères-le-Châtel data center. Launch partners include Black Forest Labs, BNP Paribas, and Thales; CEO Arthur Mensch said Mistral is expanding "from an AI company doing software to a cloud company."

item.title: Foxconn and Bull to build Nvidia Vera Rubin NVL72 servers in France
item.description: Foxconn, French computing firm Bull, and Nvidia formalized a partnership at VivaTech under which Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale AI supercomputer components will be produced at Foxconn's Czech Republic facilities, then assembled and validated at Bull's factory in Angers, France, backed by an initial investment exceeding €120 million. Foxconn also showed a wheeled humanoid robot performing dual-arm factory assembly tasks, its formal European robotics debut.

item.title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang pledges 10x European AI compute capacity
item.description: In his GTC Paris keynote opening VivaTech 2026, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the company will increase European AI computing capacity "by a factor of ten" over the next two years, framing AI infrastructure buildout as a generational industrial shift. The keynote coincided with multiple sovereign-AI infrastructure deals announced on the show floor.

item.title: China's embodied-AI financing surpasses 20 billion yuan for 2026, over 30 disclosed deals
item.description: Cumulative financing for China's embodied-intelligence and humanoid-robotics sector passed 20 billion yuan (roughly $2.8 billion) in 2026, spread across more than 30 disclosed funding rounds, already exceeding full-year 2025 totals. The surge coincides with Unitree's IPO progress and rising state-utility robot procurement, with some estimates citing China's broader 2026 robotics investment ambitions reaching into the hundreds of billions of dollars.

item.title: Anthropic Opens Seoul Office, Lands NAVER, Samsung, and LG Across Korea
item.description: Anthropic opened its third Asia-Pacific office in Seoul, led by KiYoung Choi, alongside a wave of Korean enterprise deals: NAVER is deploying Claude Code across its entire engineering organization, Samsung SDS is rolling out Claude Cowork and Claude Code across Samsung Electronics, Nexon is using Claude Code for live-service game development, and LG CNS is deploying Claude across the LG Group. Anthropic also signed an MOU with Korea's Ministry of Science and ICT and will give up to 60 researchers at KAIST, Korea University, Yonsei, and POSTECH access to Claude.

item.title: Sweden's Legora and Ironclad Launch Bidirectional 'AI-to-AI' Legal Integration
item.description: Stockholm-founded legal-AI platform Legora and US contract-lifecycle AI platform Ironclad announced a bidirectional integration connecting Legora's legal research and analysis AI with Ironclad's contracting AI. Legora CEO Max Junestrand said it is the first time two legal AI platforms have been connected this deeply and in both directions; rollout is phased to mutual customers.

item.title: SpaceX to Acquire Cursor Maker Anysphere for $60 Billion in All-Stock Deal
item.description: SpaceX signed a merger agreement on June 16, 2026 to acquire Anysphere, maker of the Cursor coding agent, in an all-stock deal valuing the startup at $60 billion; SpaceX subsidiary X67 Inc. will merge with Anysphere, which becomes a wholly owned subsidiary. The deal follows an option SpaceX secured in April 2026 and is expected to close in Q3 2026 pending regulatory approval. Cursor has scaled to roughly $2.6 billion in annualized revenue since its 2022 founding.

item.title: EU Parliament Casts Final Vote on AI Act Omnibus, Adds Nudifier Ban
item.description: The European Parliament voted 423-57-174 on June 16, 2026 to give final approval to the Digital Omnibus package amending the AI Act, postponing high-risk AI obligations to December 2027 (standalone systems) and August 2028 (embedded systems), and delaying watermarking obligations to December 2026. The text adds a new ban on non-consensual AI 'nudification' apps following the earlier Grok-related scandal. Formal Council adoption is expected before the original August 2, 2026 deadline.

item.title: Microsoft routes GitHub traffic through AWS as AI coding agents overwhelm Azure
item.description: Microsoft confirmed on June 16 that it is offloading GitHub traffic to Amazon Web Services after AI coding-agent activity pushed the platform past its reliability limits, with GitHub processing roughly 275 million commits per week (14 billion annualized, versus 1 billion in 2025). GitHub logged nine service-degrading incidents in May 2026 alone, with June availability tracking below 99%.

item.title: Swedish AI patent platform Lightbringer raises $10M Series A
item.description: Malmö-based legaltech startup Lightbringer raised €8.6M ($10M) in a Series A co-led by London's 6 Degrees Capital and Amsterdam's Newion to fund US expansion. Founded in 2023, the AI-native patent platform cuts filing timelines from roughly two months to a few days and has served 200+ deep-tech companies across 17 countries.

item.title: DeepSeek closes first-ever external funding round at $7.4B, state fund alone gets voting rights
item.description: DeepSeek closed its maiden external fundraise on June 16, 2026, raising roughly 51 billion yuan ($7.4 billion) at a post-money valuation above $50 billion. Under an unusual structure, commercial investors including Tencent (~10B yuan) and CATL (~5B yuan) accepted a five-year lock-up and gave up voting rights, while China's National AI Industry Investment Fund retained full voting control; founder Liang Wenfeng personally contributed the largest single stake (~20B yuan).

item.title: SoftBank launches OpenAI-powered "Patching as a Service" for Japan's critical infrastructure
item.description: SoftBank Group and SB OAI Japan announced "Patching as a Service" on June 16, 2026 in Tokyo, an AI-driven cybersecurity offering targeting Japan's roughly 3,000 largest critical-infrastructure companies (power, transport, airports). The service uses OpenAI's models to scan systems for vulnerabilities and produce remediation plans, with human teams handling implementation; the launch team of ~50 people is slated to scale to 1,000, and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman appeared via video at the event.

item.title: SpaceX Files $60B All-Stock Deal to Acquire Cursor Maker Anysphere
item.description: SpaceX exercised a call option and filed a binding merger agreement with the SEC to acquire Anysphere, maker of the AI coding assistant Cursor, in an all-stock deal valuing the company at $60 billion — up from the $50 billion valuation Cursor was reportedly seeking in funding talks weeks earlier. The deal is expected to close in Q3 2026 pending regulatory approval and is among the largest acquisitions of a VC-backed AI startup to date.

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI Launch AI Cybersecurity Service for Japan's Top 3,000 Infrastructure Firms
item.description: SoftBank Group, SoftBank Corp, and the 50:50 SB OAI Japan joint venture with OpenAI launched an AI-powered vulnerability-assessment and remediation service called 'Patching as a Service,' targeting Japan's roughly 3,000 most critical infrastructure companies across airports, power, and transportation. SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son called Japan's cyber vulnerability 'a crisis,' and the joint venture plans to scale its team from about 50 to 1,000 staff.

item.title: Salesforce to Acquire Fin (formerly Intercom) for $3.6 Billion
item.description: Salesforce announced on June 15, 2026 a definitive agreement to acquire Fin, the AI customer-service agent company formerly known as Intercom, for approximately $3.6 billion. Fin's AI Agent, powered by its proprietary Apex model, resolves customer queries end-to-end across chat, email, WhatsApp, SMS, phone, and Slack; the deal is the largest acquisition of an agentic customer-experience provider to date and is expected to close in Salesforce's fiscal Q4 2027 pending regulatory approval.

item.title: Varonis Discloses 'SearchLeak' One-Click Data-Theft Chain in Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Varonis Threat Labs disclosed SearchLeak (CVE-2026-42824) on June 15, 2026, a three-stage attack chaining a parameter-to-prompt injection, an HTML-rendering race condition, and a Bing SSRF-enabled content-security-policy bypass to exfiltrate emails, passwords, calendar entries, and SharePoint/OneDrive files via a single malicious-link click in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Microsoft rated it critical (CVSS 6.5; NVD scored it 7.5) and patched it server-side in early June 2026 with no customer action required; no in-the-wild exploitation was found.

item.title: China's State Grid earmarks $1B for 8,500 embodied-AI robots in 2026
item.description: China's State Grid Corporation set aside 6.8 billion yuan (about $1 billion) to procure roughly 8,500 embodied-intelligence robots in 2026 for power-grid operations and maintenance. Including similar plans from China Southern Power Grid and other utilities, industry estimates put total embodied-AI investment in the power sector above 10 billion yuan this year.

item.title: India's Sarvam AI becomes a unicorn with $234M Series B led by HCLTech
item.description: Sarvam AI announced on June 15, 2026 that it raised $234 million in the first close of a planned $300 million Series B, reaching a post-money valuation of $1.5 billion. HCLTech and Bessemer Venture Partners led the round, with existing backers Khosla Ventures and Peak XV Partners also participating; funds will go toward training Sarvam's next frontier model for agentic, coding, and cybersecurity use cases.

item.title: OpenAI launches Partner Network with $150M investment
item.description: OpenAI introduced its first formal global partner program on June 14, backing it with $150 million to help consulting firms and systems integrators deploy enterprise AI, with a target of 300,000 certified consultants by end of 2026. Launch partners include Accenture, Bain, BCG, McKinsey and PwC across three tiers (Select, Advanced, Elite).

item.title: Google in talks with Samsung to manufacture part of next-gen "Icefish" TPU
item.description: Google is discussing having Samsung Foundry produce the I/O die for its 10th-generation TPU (codenamed Icefish) on a 2nm process, while TSMC continues making the 1.4nm compute die, as reported June 14. The move reflects hyperscalers hedging against TSMC's AI-driven capacity crunch, with mass production targeted for 2028.

item.title: Zhipu AI releases GLM-5.2 with 1M-token context, fully open-sourced under MIT license
item.description: Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released GLM-5.2, a Mixture-of-Experts model with roughly 744B total parameters and ~40B active per query, expanding context from GLM-5.1's 200K tokens to 1 million. The model shipped first via the GLM Coding Plan with day-one support for eight agentic IDEs, with open weights released under an MIT license days later; Zhipu's Hong Kong-listed shares jumped as much as 48% intraday on the news.

item.title: Zhipu trains GLM-5.2 entirely on Huawei chips, without Nvidia hardware
item.description: Zhipu AI confirmed GLM-5.2 was trained exclusively on Huawei silicon, marking one of the first frontier-class open-weight models trained without any Nvidia GPUs. The disclosure came alongside the model's June 2026 release and underscores progress in China's domestic AI-chip stack under continued US export controls.

item.title: Ramp AI Index: Anthropic Reaches 41% of US Businesses, Overtakes OpenAI in Enterprise Adoption
item.description: Ramp's AI Index, based on corporate card spending data from tens of thousands of US businesses, shows Anthropic's business adoption climbing to 41% in June 2026 — up 2.5 points in a month — making it the most-adopted AI provider in US enterprises for the first time, ahead of OpenAI. Anthropic has grown from under 8% adoption in April 2025 to 41% in June 2026, while OpenAI's adoption has been roughly flat over the past year.

item.title: Ukraine's AI-Guided Drones Strike Russian Logistics Up to 150km Behind the Front
item.description: Ukraine is fielding AI-guided strike drones with autonomous, jam-resistant targeting that can hit fuel depots, ammunition dumps, and command posts up to 150km behind Russian lines, contributing to a 40% month-over-month jump in strikes on Russian tanker trucks in May. Separately, Ukrainian firm MaXon Systems deployed an autonomous air-defense system that automatically detects, identifies, and locks onto Shahed drones, automating roughly 95% of interceptions while keeping final engagement decisions under human control.

item.title: SpaceX Prices IPO at $135/Share, Debuts on Nasdaq as 'SPCX' in Largest-Ever Stock Offering
item.description: SpaceX priced its initial public offering at $135 per share for 555,555,555 Class A shares — implying a valuation near $1.75 trillion — and began trading on Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX on June 12, 2026, opening around $150 and closing up roughly 19% at $161. The offering, the largest in stock-market history, was led by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and a syndicate of major banks, and arrives as OpenAI and Anthropic also pursue public listings.

item.title: US Export-Control Directive Forces Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said it received a US government directive on June 12, 2026 at 5:21pm ET ordering it to suspend all access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals worldwide — including Anthropic's own foreign employees — citing export-control concerns after the government learned of a method to 'jailbreak' Fable 5. Anthropic stated it believes the directive reflects a misunderstanding and is working with the government to restore access as quickly as possible, while partners including AWS began revoking access to comply.

item.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K2.7-Code, a 1T-Parameter Open-Source Coding Model
item.description: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7-Code on June 12, 2026, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model (32B active, 384 experts) with a 256K-token context window under a Modified MIT license. The model scores +21.8% on Moonshot's Kimi Code Bench v2 and +11.0% on Program Bench versus K2.6 while using roughly 30% fewer reasoning tokens. Weights are available on Hugging Face and ModelScope; API access via the Moonshot platform is priced at $0.95/$4.00 per million input/output tokens.

item.title: OpenAI Retires GPT-5.2 from ChatGPT, Migrates Conversations to GPT-5.5
item.description: As of June 12, 2026, GPT-5.2 Instant, Thinking, and Pro are no longer available in ChatGPT, following OpenAI's standard 90-day sunset window after a successor model ships. Existing conversations that used GPT-5.2 automatically continue on the corresponding GPT-5.5 model. OpenAI separately confirmed GPT-4.5 will be retired from ChatGPT on June 27, 2026, and o3 on August 26, 2026.

item.title: AI Lab Leaders to Join G7 Leaders' Summit in Évian-les-Bains
item.description: Bloomberg reported on June 12, 2026 that executives from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and Mistral AI — including Dario Amodei, Sam Altman, Demis Hassabis, and Arthur Mensch — are expected to attend the G7 Leaders' Summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, running June 15–17, 2026. France is crafting an agenda that places AI alongside broader economic and security topics for the gathering of G7 heads of state.

item.title: Mistral AI in Early Talks to Raise €3B at €20B Valuation
item.description: Bloomberg reported on June 12, 2026 that French AI lab Mistral AI is in early discussions to raise about €3 billion ($3.5 billion), which would value the company at roughly €20 billion ($23.15 billion) — nearly double its €11.7 billion Series C valuation from September 2025. Mistral has set a target of surpassing $1 billion in annual recurring revenue by the end of 2026; terms are still early and could change.
